Land crude import weakness (Canada is low on oil inventories) + weak implied US crude oil production = bigger draw surprise

tweets.text:
Honestly shouldn't have posted this.

Dealt with a lot of shit the last month, but I understand why.

All of the criticism was fair.

I believe that the fear of how others will react to changing bias' when you've been vocal about something for so long is why so many traders get trapped

That feeling isn't isolated to large accounts like me.

Everyone deals with it on some scale - whether it's with friends, family, coworkers, etc.

It's just most apparent concentrated on places like this, which is why I found it so important to share.

The market eventually humbles everyone.

Don't let your pride stop you from being objective.

tweets.text:
I appreciate it man, but I definitely haven't been speaking it all year.

For way too long I was the most vocal bull on the timeline, and I liked Saylor pressing the buy button because it fit my bias. I believed crypto had to eventually catch up to the AI / tech trade, and someone aping billions felt good to see.

It wasn't until the end of May when I started seeing Saylor's lies and finally woke up and realized how bad things were.

Took a lot to put aside my bullish bias and look at the situation objectively. Was definitely humbling.

And I believe a lot of people are in the same boat, which is why I've been so vocal the last month.

tweets.text:
$STRC just made new all time lows again in the low 80s.

Honestly think this might get a bit worse than just going to the mid 70s.

Expect it to more closely follow the path of what we've seen with STRD, STRK, etc.

Strategy still has the opportunity to save their holders and assets from absolutely cannibalizing themselves, but as I've been saying the last several weeks - it'll take some humility and doing something drastic.

e.g. sell 50-100K+ BTC now and put $10B+ on their balance sheet rather than waiting until it dips into the mid 30s... the sooner they sell - the less they have to sell.

tweets.text: Even if the Fed is serious about bringing inflation down to 2% with aggressive rate hikes and QT, once the stock market accepts that and crashes, the Fed will do a 180. A stock market crash, plus a real estate crash, threatens not only a recession but another financial crisis.
tweets.full_text: Even if the Fed is serious about bringing inflation down to 2% with aggressive rate hikes and QT, once the stock market accepts that and crashes, the Fed will do a 180. A stock market crash, plus a real estate crash, threatens not only a recession but another financial crisis.

tweets.text: If precious metals traders are right about how aggressive the Fed will get to crush inflation, the stock market should be crashing. If stock traders are right that the Fed is more bark than bite when it comes to rate hikes, gold prices should be soaring. They both can't be right.
tweets.full_text: If precious metals traders are right about how aggressive the Fed will get to crush inflation, the stock market should be crashing. If stock traders are right that the Fed is more bark than bite when it comes to rate hikes, gold prices should be soaring. They both can't be right.

tweets.text: Gold traders are pricing in aggressive rate hikes, but stock investors appear to still be pricing in cuts. The latter may be correct, but not because rate cuts are appropriate, but because they will be a political necessity. Those cuts will be more bullish for gold than stocks.
tweets.full_text: Gold traders are pricing in aggressive rate hikes, but stock investors appear to still be pricing in cuts. The latter may be correct, but not because rate cuts are appropriate, but because they will be a political necessity. Those cuts will be more bullish for gold than stocks.
